Maria Clara, can I ask for your advice? I have a few doubts before Seoul.
Of course. In my opinion, you should ask early, that is always the best option.
My visa might not be ready in time. Should I travel anyway?
No, you shouldn't travel without it. You could call the embassy today and explain the situation.
Good idea. And the rector, should I invite him to the opening session?
Yes, I recommend you invite him. But you might want to confirm the date first.
And the interpreter? She might cancel again.
Then prepare a second option. Better to be safe than sorry, I advise you to have a second plan.

Hi Maria Clara, it is Bruno. A quick question before the Seoul trip. I need your advice. First, the documents. I have my passport, but I might not have the visa in time. Should I travel anyway, or should I wait? Second, the meeting. In my opinion, we could invite the rector to the opening session, but I am not sure. What do you recommend? Third, the interpreter. She might cancel again, and I do not want to take a big risk. You always say, better to be safe than sorry, so should I prepare a second plan? Please call me back with your advice. Thank you.

Good afternoon. This is a short message from the institutional travel office, with some advice for the delegation to Seoul. Please listen before you travel. First, your documents. You should carry your passport and your visa together, and you shouldn't put them in your luggage. Keep them with you. Second, the money. We recommend you take a little cash in local currency, because you might need it at the airport. Third, the schedule. You should confirm every appointment one day before, and you shouldn't change the times without a good reason. And one more thing. You might feel tired after the long flight, so we advise you to rest before the first meeting. It is always better to be safe than sorry. Have a good trip.
